context:
Section 7 (Conclusions), page 27

Nevertheless, there are many other unresolved stability issues in NEPs. For instance, is the I-property also necessary for the isolated calmness of \(S_{\mathrm{KKT}}\)? In the context of NLP, the answer is positive, where the I-property actually reduces to the SOSC (Dontchev and Rockafellar [9]) under the SONC for optimality.
title: Necessity of the I-property for isolated calmness of the KKT solution mapping \(S_{\mathrm{KKT}}\) in Nash equilibrium problems
background_latex:
Consider a Nash equilibrium problem (NEP) with \(N\) players and canonical perturbation parameter \(p=(p_1,\dots,p_N)\) with \(p_k=(u_k,v_k,w_k)\). Let \(x=(x_1,\dots,x_N)\in\mathbb{R}^n\) denote the players' strategies and \(\lambda=(\lambda_1,\dots,\lambda_N)\in\mathbb{R}^m\) the associated KKT multipliers, with \(\lambda_k\in\mathbb{R}^{m_k}\). Let \(S_{\mathrm{KKT}}:\mathbb{R}^{m+n+d}\rightrightarrows\mathbb{R}^{n+m}\) be the KKT solution mapping that assigns to each \(p\) the set of KKT triples \((x,\lambda)\) satisfying the generalized equation system (KKT conditions for all players with equality/inequality constraints).
Fix a reference KKT point \((\bar p,\bar x,\bar\lambda)\in\mathrm{gph}\,S_{\mathrm{KKT}}\). For each player \(k\), define index sets of active inequalities at \((\bar p,\bar x,\bar\lambda)\):
- \(I_1^k := \{i\in\{s_k+1,\dots,m_k\}: \bar\lambda_i^k>0= g_i^k(\bar x_k;\bar w_k)-\bar u_i^k\}\cup\{1,\dots,s_k\}\),
- \(I_2^k := \{i\in\{s_k+1,\dots,m_k\}: \bar\lambda_i^k=0= g_i^k(\bar x_k;\bar w_k)-\bar u_i^k\}\),
- \(I_3^k := \{i\in\{s_k+1,\dots,m_k\}: \bar\lambda_i^k=0> g_i^k(\bar x_k;\bar w_k)-\bar u_i^k\}\).
Let \(I_1:=\cup_{k=1}^N I_1^k\) and \(I_2:=\cup_{k=1}^N I_2^k\). Define the cone
\[K(I_1,I_2):=\{y=(y_1,\dots,y_N)\in\mathbb{R}^n:\nabla_{x_k}g_i^k(\bar x_k;\bar w_k)^T y_k=0\ \forall i\in I_1^k,\ \nabla_{x_k}g_i^k(\bar x_k;\bar w_k)^T y_k\le 0\ \forall i\in I_2^k,\ k=1,\dots,N\}.\]
Let \(L_k(x_k,x_{-k},\lambda_k;w_k)\) denote player \(k\)'s Lagrangian, and let \(\nabla^2_{x_k x_i} L_k(\bar x_k,\bar x_{-k},\bar\lambda_k;\bar w_k)\) denote its mixed Hessian blocks. The paper defines the I-property at \((\bar p,\bar x,\bar\lambda)\) on \(K(I_1,I_2)\) by: for any \(y\in K(I_1,I_2)\), if for every \(k=1,\dots,N\),
\[\sum_{i=1}^N y_k^T\nabla^2_{x_k x_i} L_k(\bar x_k,\bar x_{-k},\bar\lambda_k;\bar w_k) y_i = 0,\]
then \(y=0\).
Isolated calmness of \(S_{\mathrm{KKT}}\) at \(\bar p\) for \((\bar x,\bar\lambda)\) means there exist \(\kappa\ge 0\) and neighborhoods \(U\) of \(\bar p\), \(V\) of \((\bar x,\bar\lambda)\) such that
\[S_{\mathrm{KKT}}(p)\cap V \subset \{(\bar x,\bar\lambda)\}+\kappa\|p-\bar p\|\,B\quad\forall p\in U.\]
The paper proves that (under an SMFCQ-type assumption) the I-property is sufficient for isolated calmness.
problem_definition_latex:
Determine whether the I-property at \((\bar p,\bar x,\bar\lambda)\) on \(K(I_1,I_2)\) is necessary for \(S_{\mathrm{KKT}}\) to be isolated calm at \(\bar p\) for \((\bar x,\bar\lambda)\). Equivalently, either
- prove that if \(S_{\mathrm{KKT}}\) is isolated calm at \(\bar p\) for \((\bar x,\bar\lambda)\) (under appropriate standard constraint qualification assumptions, e.g. SMFCQ for each player at \((\bar p_k,\bar x_k,\bar\lambda_k)\)), then the I-property must hold, or
- provide a counterexample NEP and KKT point \((\bar p,\bar x,\bar\lambda)\) such that \(S_{\mathrm{KKT}}\) is isolated calm at \(\bar p\) for \((\bar x,\bar\lambda)\) but the I-property fails on \(K(I_1,I_2)\).
latex:

The paper proves that (under an SMFCQ-type assumption) the I-property is sufficient for isolated calmness.

Determine whether the I-property at \((\bar p,\bar x,\bar\lambda)\) on \(K(I_1,I_2)\) is necessary for \(S_{\mathrm{KKT}}\) to be isolated calm at \(\bar p\) for \((\bar x,\bar\lambda)\). Equivalently, either
- prove that if \(S_{\mathrm{KKT}}\) is isolated calm at \(\bar p\) for \((\bar x,\bar\lambda)\) (under appropriate standard constraint qualification assumptions, e.g. SMFCQ for each player at \((\bar p_k,\bar x_k,\bar\lambda_k)\)), then the I-property must hold, or
- provide a counterexample NEP and KKT point \((\bar p,\bar x,\bar\lambda)\) such that \(S_{\mathrm{KKT}}\) is isolated calm at \(\bar p\) for \((\bar x,\bar\lambda)\) but the I-property fails on \(K(I_1,I_2)\).
